Rbebelm 0.3.6-0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Getting started
    • Late-interaction retrieval with ColBERT
    • Retrieval embeddings with EmbeddingGemma
    • Agents and tools
    • Backends and webR
    • Benchmark harness

Late-interaction retrieval with ColBERT

Source: vignettes/colbert.Rmd
colbert.Rmd
library(Rbebelm)
weights_file <- Sys.getenv("COLBERT_WEIGHTS_FILE", "/root/bebelm/LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M-Q4_K_M.gguf")
stopifnot(file.exists(weights_file))
model <- colbert_model_load(weights_file, num_threads = 2)

LFM2.5-ColBERT-350M is a retrieval-trained, non-causal encoder. Unlike a dense embedding model, it preserves a matrix of vectors: one L2-normalized 128-dimensional row for each query or retained document token. Retrieval uses ColBERT’s late-interaction MaxSim operation:

score⁡(q,d)=∑imaxjqi⊤dj. \operatorname{score}(q, d) = \sum_i \max_j q_i^\top d_j.

Query and document contracts

The native profile implements the release contract: [Q] and [D] prefixes, a 32-position query (including learned PAD expansion positions), a 512-position document, published document punctuation filtering, a learned dense projection, and per-token L2 normalization. These are model semantics, not caller options.

query <- colbert_encode_query(model, "capital of Mali")
document <- colbert_encode_document(
  model,
  "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali."
)

colbert_embeddings_info(query)
## $kind
## [1] "query"
## 
## $tokens
## [1] 32
## 
## $dimensions
## [1] 128
colbert_embeddings_info(document)
## $kind
## [1] "document"
## 
## $tokens
## [1] 13
## 
## $dimensions
## [1] 128
head(colbert_embedding_ids(document))
## [1]     1 64401   543   831  6762   856
head(colbert_embedding_vectors(document))
##              [,1]        [,2]      [,3]        [,4]         [,5]         [,6]
## [1,]  0.048641685 -0.02103253 0.1053516 -0.17262876  0.001319423  0.029039390
## [2,]  0.051768061 -0.01189769 0.1392703 -0.05095049  0.106721312 -0.051922910

## [4,] -0.017137622  0.01176232 0.1108944 -0.04544714 -0.044595007 -0.002917319

## [1,] -0.137631997 -0.07590140 0.07043449  0.09548700
## [2,] -0.045175046 -0.04678767 0.08605231 -0.01167286
## [3,] -0.059616014 -0.07521211 0.07524399  0.06161517
## [4,]  0.001191781 -0.05149571 0.11064458  0.05703056
## [5,]  0.001064059 -0.01179361 0.12184162  0.04562062
## [6,] -0.131241262 -0.07886329 0.05961343  0.08511575
colbert_maxsim(query, document)
## [1] 30.60933

Exact candidate ranking

colbert_rank() is a small exact scorer for re-ranking candidate documents. It deliberately does not claim to be an ANN or compressed late-interaction index; a production corpus should be indexed by a system that preserves this same model and preprocessing contract.

colbert_rank(
  model,
  "capital of Mali",
  c(
    mali = "Bamako is the capital and largest city of Mali.",
    italy = "Rome is the capital city of Italy.",
    desert = "The Sahara is a desert in northern Africa."
  )
)
## <ColBERT MaxSim ranking>
##     mali    italy   desert 
## 30.60933 29.99548 29.56865

On this page
